lede: A look at March 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Abby Ryder Fortson and Patrick Patterson (basketball), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hlaingthaya massacre
events.desc: Burmese security forces kill at least 65 civilians in the Hlaingthaya massacre.

events.title: Escondida
events.desc: Escondida in Chile's Atacama Desert – which was to become the worlds most productive copper mine – is officially inaugurated.

events.title: Johnson South Reef skirmish
events.desc: In the Johnson South Reef Skirmish Chinese forces defeat Vietnamese forces in an altercation over control of one of the Spratly Islands.

events.title: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007
events.desc: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007 crashes during final approach near Warsaw, Poland, killing 87 people, including a 14-man American boxing team.

events.title: Royal Air Force
events.desc: The R.A.F. drop the Grand Slam bomb in action for the first time, on a railway viaduct near Bielefeld, Germany.

events.title: Penicillin
events.desc: Anne Miller becomes the first American patient to be treated with penicillin, under the care of Orvan Hess and John Bumstead.

events.title: Alam Ara
events.desc: Alam Ara, India's first talking film, is released.

events.title: Gold Standard Act
events.desc: The Gold Standard Act is ratified, placing the United States currency on the gold standard.

events.title: The Mikado
events.desc: The Mikado, a light opera by W. S. Gilbert and Arthur Sullivan, receives its first public performance at the Savoy Theatre in London.

events.title: Petite messe solennelle
events.desc: Rossini's Petite messe solennelle is first performed, by twelve singers, two pianists and a harmonium player in a mansion in Paris.

births.name: François Morel (composer)
births.line: Canadian pianist, composer, conductor, and educator (died 2018)

births.name: John Lane (publisher)
births.line: English publisher, co-founded The Bodley Head (died 1925)

deaths.text: Alan Simpson (American politician), United States senator from Wyoming
Suranimala Rajapaksha, Sri Lankan lawyer and politician

observances.text: Nanakshahi New Year, first day of the month of Chet (Sikhism)
